---
title: "Teaching"
pagetitle: "Teaching — Bingjin Xue"
---

## Current courses

::: {.course}
### ECON 898 / 720 — Machine Learning for Economists
<div class="term">Graduate · Spring 2026</div>

An applied course introducing modern predictive modeling and machine learning methods to graduate economists. Topics include regularized regression, tree-based methods, neural networks, and the use of ML for causal inference (double/debiased ML, causal forests). Emphasis on econometric intuition and reproducible workflows.

[Syllabus](assets/econ898-syllabus.pdf){aria-label="Syllabus PDF"}
:::

::: {.course}
### ECO 565 — Predictive Modeling
<div class="term">Undergraduate · Fall 2025</div>

Undergraduate introduction to predictive modeling. Covers linear and logistic regression, resampling, model selection, and an introduction to tree-based methods. Projects use real economic data.

[Syllabus](assets/eco565-syllabus.pdf){aria-label="Syllabus PDF"}
:::

::: {.course}
### ECO 402 — Principles of Microeconomics
<div class="term">Undergraduate · recurring</div>

Standard undergraduate microeconomics. Producer and consumer theory, market structure, welfare analysis, externalities.
:::

## Interactive demos

Browser-based visualizations built for classroom use. Each runs locally — no install, no accounts.

- [**Linear regression visualizer**](demos/regression-visualizer.html) — drag points to see how OLS reacts. Live residuals, R², and coefficient estimates.
- [**Omitted variable bias**](demos/ovb-demo.html) — toggle the omitted variable on and off and watch the bias in the estimated coefficient move in the predicted direction.
- [**Cobb-Douglas utility maximization**](demos/cobb-douglas.html) — interactive budget constraint with Slutsky decomposition into income and substitution effects.

## Advising

I am happy to talk with UNH undergraduates interested in research in applied microeconomics, health economics, or econometrics. Email me to set up a time.
